taxonomy: Brassica intricata is classified as a member of the Brassicaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as mustard or cabbage family. Within this family, Brassica intricata is further classified as a part of the genus Brassica, which includes many other species of cruciferous plants such as broccoli, cauliflower, and kale. The genus Brassica is itself a member of the order Brassicales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the shepard's purse and the watercress. The order Brassicales is part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as the angiosperms. The angiosperms are a group of plants that produce flowers and seeds, and are thought to have evolved around 130 million years ago during the Cretaceous period.
